name: Poem
classification_notes: The page content is a live specimen editor showing the typeface 'Poem' used across a chaotic stream of user-generated text samples. Insufficient detail is available to determine a more precise subcategory beyond display.
summary: A typeface from Or Type shown through a live, chaotic specimen editor populated by user-generated text samples.
description: The page presents Poem via a real-time specimen editor described as 'for your eyes only', filled with a rolling stream of user-submitted text — brand names, song lyrics, place names and nonsense strings. This approach to presentation is itself a design statement, foregrounding how the typeface performs in unpredictable, uncontrolled contexts. Beyond the specimen interface, structural details such as weights, italics and language support are not disclosed on this page.
foundry_blurb: Or Type presents Poem through an interactive specimen editor marked 'Off Air', inviting visitors to see the typeface tested live against a constant stream of real user input.

name: Or Lemmen
classification_notes: The family's range of upright and italic weights alongside condensed and mono subfamilies suggests a workhorse grotesque sans-serif designed for extended typographic systems.
summary: A versatile grotesque sans from Or Type offered in upright and italic cuts across five weights, with condensed and monospaced subfamilies.
description: Or Lemmen covers five weights from Thin to Bold, each with a matching italic, and extends into Condensed and Mono subfamilies for a total of thirty styles. The condensed cuts mirror the weight range of the upright family, and the mono variant adds five weights with italics for code or tabular use. The breadth of the family makes it suited to editorial and interface work requiring a consistent typographic voice across varied contexts.
foundry_blurb: Or Type offers Or Lemmen as a large family spanning regular, condensed and mono subfamilies, with individual styles priced at €80 each and the full family available for €500.

name: Similar
classification_notes: The specimen page and naming conventions suggest a display-oriented typeface with a playful, conceptual character; the doppelgänger and twin themes point to a design exploring mirroring or duplication as a formal device.
summary: A display typeface from Or Type built around the concept of similarity, doubling and doppelgängers, with a specimen that leans heavily into its own thematic territory.
description: Or Type's Similar takes its conceptual cue from twins, synonyms and doppelgängers, with a specimen that moves through mythology, poetry and pop culture to frame the design's character. The page draws on Heine's Der Doppelgänger and Babylonian astronomy to set a tone that is literary and slightly uncanny. Weight and language details are not disclosed on the specimen page, suggesting a focused, single-style release aimed at display use.
foundry_blurb: Or Type present Similar through a specimen steeped in the language of doubles and likenesses, exploring synonyms, twins and alter egos from Castor and Pollux to Ziggy Stardust, framing the typeface as something similar but distinct.

name: Forzata
classification_notes: The specimen's strong vertical stress, bracketed serifs, and high contrast suggest a classical Italian display serif tradition, used throughout at large sizes for headlines and short-form editorial text.
summary: A high-contrast display serif from Or Type drawing on Italian typographic tradition, shown through a specimen steeped in automotive and Renaissance cultural reference.
description: The specimen pairs tightly set Italian motifs, Alfa Romeo, Ferrari, Lancia, Firenze, with passages of Renaissance history, suggesting a typeface built for editorial and cultural contexts that want classical Italian gravitas. Its contrast and bracketed serifs place it squarely in the display register, suited to headlines, posters and short bursts of text at large sizes. The aesthetic sits somewhere between the drama of Italian Baroque letterforms and the graphic vernacular of mid-century motoring print.
foundry_blurb: Or Type present Forzata through a specimen rich in Italian cultural references, from Florentine Renaissance history and Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel to Enzo Ferrari's biography and iconic Italian cars. The specimen positions the typeface as a vehicle for editorial work that draws on Italy's graphic and cultural heritage.

name: La Pontaise Champion
classification_notes: The specimen presents a bold, condensed display typeface used exclusively for sporting headlines, titles and logotype-style settings, placing it firmly in the display sans category.
summary: A condensed display sans built for sport, drawing on the visual language of French athletic typography and competition graphics.
description: Designed for large-scale sporting headlines and competition branding, the typeface takes its name from La Pontaise, the historic stadium in Lausanne. The specimen leans heavily on French sporting culture, from cycling team names to football clubs and athletics, suggesting a typeface calibrated for the energy and authority of major championship contexts. Its condensed proportions make it suited to tight editorial settings and poster-scale display work.
foundry_blurb: Or Type present La Pontaise Champion through a specimen steeped in French sport, featuring references to Roland Garros, the Tour de France, the UEFA Champions League and the Vendée Globe, alongside athletes such as Kylian Mbappé and Roger Federer. The typeface takes its name from the Pontaise stadium in Lausanne, founded in 1904.

name: Separat
classification_notes: The specimen foregrounds symmetry, reflection and mirroring as core design principles, with palindromic letterform arrangements suggesting a geometric display typeface built around bilateral symmetry. The expressive, concept-driven specimen copy positions it firmly as display rather than text.
summary: A concept-driven display typeface from Or Type that uses symmetry and reflection as its structural and visual foundation.
description: Built around the principle of bilateral symmetry, the typeface draws on geometric transformations — scaling, reflection and rotation — as a core part of its design logic. The specimen demonstrates Cyrillic support alongside Latin, with the mirror theme carried through the editorial copy referencing physics, palindromes and paired cultural icons. Suited to editorial, poster and identity work where a strong conceptual framework is needed.
foundry_blurb: Or Type frame Separat around the idea of symmetry understood as invariance and transformation, referencing everything from Noether's theorem to palindromes and cultural doubles. The specimen positions the typeface as a meditation on reflection and duality, demonstrated through both Latin and Cyrillic scripts.

name: Boogie School Serif
classification_notes: The typeface exhibits a playful, expressive character suited to display use, with a serif construction that carries a loose, informal quality consistent with display-oriented type design.
summary: A display serif from Or Type with a loose, informal character built around an education-themed specimen.
description: Boogie School Serif is a display serif with an expressive, casual personality, presented through a specimen that leans heavily into school and education imagery. The typeface carries a relaxed energy that suits editorial, poster and branding contexts where a serif with personality is needed over a workhorse text face. The specimen demonstrates strong numerals and headline settings across a range of playful copy treatments.
foundry_blurb: Or Type present Boogie School Serif through an education-themed specimen drawing on progressive pedagogy references, from Montessori to Decroly, framing the typeface within a spirit of curiosity, experimentation and learning.

name: Or Lemmen
classification_notes: The specimen's heavy references to Jugendstil, Les XX and the Belgian painter-designer Georges Lemmen suggest an art nouveau-inflected serif; the decorative, period-conscious aesthetic points to a display-oriented typeface within the serif category.
summary: A display serif inspired by the Jugendstil movement and the work of Belgian artist Georges Lemmen, designed for literary and editorial contexts.
description: Named after the Belgian designer and Les XX member Georges Lemmen, this typeface draws on the decorative sensibility of late 19th-century Jugendstil. The specimen leans into literary and cultural references, from Nietzsche to post-impressionist artists, suggesting a typeface suited to editorial, book and poster work with period character. Its visual language sits somewhere between classical book typography and ornamental fin-de-siècle design.
foundry_blurb: Or Type's specimen for Or Lemmen frames the typeface through the world of Georges Lemmen and the Belgian art movement Les XX, evoking Jugendstil aesthetics, post-impressionist art and literary culture from the 1880s and 1890s.

name: Boogie School Sans
classification_notes: Named and presented as a display sans; the 'Boogie School' branding suggests an expressive, personality-driven sans rather than a neutral workhorse.
summary: A display sans from Or Type with an expressive, characterful bent suggested by its name and branding.
description: Boogie School Sans is a display-oriented sans from Reykjavik-based Or Type. The page content was inaccessible beyond the loading screen, so full specimen details could not be retrieved. Based on available naming and context, it appears to be a personality-driven face suited to editorial or identity work.
foundry_blurb: Or Type present Boogie School Sans as part of their type library, though full specimen copy was unavailable at time of retrieval.

name: Landnáma
classification_notes: Insufficient page content to determine a precise subcategory; classified broadly as display based on the Icelandic naming convention and Or Type's general design output.
summary: A typeface from Or Type named after the Old Norse term for the settlement of Iceland, though the page failed to load sufficient detail for further description.
description: Landnáma takes its name from the medieval Icelandic sagas documenting the settlement of Iceland, suggesting a design informed by Scandinavian or historical reference. The specimen page did not render fully, leaving weights, styles and precise classification unconfirmed. Or Type is an Icelandic foundry known for considered, culturally grounded typeface design.
foundry_blurb: No foundry copy was available from the page as it did not render beyond a loading state.

name: La Pontaise
classification_notes: The specimen's heavy French cultural and editorial flavour, combined with the large display sizes and decorative character shown throughout, places this firmly as a display serif with strong heritage and poster-typography associations.
summary: A display serif from Or Type with a distinctly French editorial character, suited to luxury, cultural and gastronomic contexts.
description: The specimen leans heavily on French cultural references, from haute couture and Burgundy wine to Parisian addresses and heritage institutions, suggesting a typeface designed for high-end editorial and identity work. Its character reads as refined and period-inflected, pointing to use in luxury branding, restaurant menus, cultural publishing and poster typography. Details from the page give insufficient information to confirm weights, italics or designer.
foundry_blurb: Or Type present La Pontaise through an immersive French-language specimen drawing on themes of Parisian fashion, regional gastronomy, cultural heritage and contemporary French identity, positioning the typeface within a rich tradition of French graphic culture.

name: L10
classification_notes: The name and foundry context suggest a modernist grotesque or geometric sans-serif, though the page content is insufficient to confirm precise subcategory with certainty.
summary: A typeface from Or Type with insufficient public specimen data available at the time of retrieval.
description: The specimen page for L10 from Or Type did not load fully, returning only a placeholder interface. No weight, style or language information could be confirmed from the available content. Further details would require a fully rendered version of the specimen page.
foundry_blurb: Or Type's specimen page for L10 presents an interactive wire broadcast concept, but no typeface details were accessible from the loaded content.
